xPosts.content: We're facing an LLM bubble, not a broader AI bubble. The industry is obsessed with building one massive model when we should be focusing on practical applications.
xPosts.highlights: Clem Delangue distinguishes between an LLM bubble and a broader AI bubble, arguing that the hype is concentrated on large language models rather than AI as a whole.
xPosts.worthReading: Provides a nuanced perspective on AI market dynamics from a leading open-source AI CEO.

xPosts.content: Is it time we stop using the word AI for everything and instead use words like 'chatbots'?
xPosts.highlights: Delangue questions the overuse of the term 'AI' and suggests more specific terms like 'chatbots'.
xPosts.worthReading: Highlights a semantic debate in the AI industry about precise terminology.

xPosts.content: Local open-weight AI on a laptop has been improving more than twice as fast as Moore's Law! Between May 2024 and May 2026, the most expensive MacBook Pro you could buy stayed at 128 GB of unified memory. The hardware ceiling barely moved. But the smartest open-weight model
